feedback.text: First of all, thank you EU for everything. Every time I see Americans getting ravaged by unregulated companies while the EU implements some new stranglehold on them my heart gets a little warmer. My feedback on the Digital Fairness Act is to please include video games as protected works of art, similar to cinema, literature, and other art forms. Video games can be a beautiful combination of so many artists' handiwork, the fact that the current landscape allows game companies to simply push a button and destroy everyone's ability to appreciate and enjoy the artwork should be stopped as soon as possible. I also beg of you to ignore lobbyist groups, especially ones that speak out without first consulting the companies that they are supposed to be representing. The consumers of video games want this movement passed, and game development companies - although they are stuggling currently - will figure their shit out eventually.
